title: One Fish Two Fish Blowfish Blue Fish

jokes.text: Isn't there anything faster than a microwave?
jokes.setup: Homer impatiently waiting for microwave to finish
jokes.punchline: Asking for something faster than instant cooking
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer impatience, well-integrated character comedy with good economy

jokes.text: I've got a dried-out end piece with your name on it.
jokes.setup: Trying to make unappetizing food sound appealing
jokes.punchline: The 'special' piece is literally the worst part
jokes.explanation: Good domestic comedy setup with Homer's misguided enthusiasm

jokes.text: Thursday. Meat loaf night. As it was, is now, and ever shall be.
jokes.setup: Describing their rigid dinner schedule
jokes.punchline: Using biblical/eternal language for mundane meatloaf routine
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of sacred language with mundane routine, very quotable

jokes.text: Who said that? Nobody wants that.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting he be open-minded and try new things
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt rejection of self-improvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - resistance to change with comic bluntness

jokes.text: We haven't missed pork chop night since the pig scare in '87!
jokes.setup: Resistance to changing Friday dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Referencing an absurd historical event affecting their dinner routine
jokes.explanation: Great background world-building joke with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: Fine! We'll go to Mars!
jokes.setup: Marge wants to go anywhere but hamburgers or pizza
jokes.punchline: Homer's ridiculous overreaction to limited options
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer escalation to absurdity, good character integration

jokes.text: The playground has the facts right but missed the point.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if sushi is raw fish like playground says
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sophisticated dismissal of playground wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - intellectual superiority delivered with child-like authority

jokes.text: If I said no the first time, would I say yes the second time? / Maybe on the ninety-ninth time. / Try me.
jokes.setup: Homer's logical argument about consistency
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately exploiting the loophole
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline structure showing Bart's quick thinking against Homer's flawed logic

jokes.text: Please, Dad? / Okay!
jokes.setup: Homer just said he wouldn't change his mind
jokes.punchline: Immediately caves on the very next ask
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Homer's complete lack of willpower, excellent economy

jokes.text: This is our karaoke bar. Now it is empty... but soon it will be full of drunken Japanese businessmen.
jokes.setup: Introducing the restaurant's karaoke section
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact prediction of stereotypical clientele
jokes.explanation: Relies on cultural stereotypes, somewhat cheap humor but delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Sushi Surprise, a bit of everything. Very non-threatening.
jokes.setup: Family unsure about being at sushi restaurant
jokes.punchline: Restaurant worker's diplomatic description for nervous customers
jokes.explanation: Good service industry humor with the diplomatic 'non-threatening' description

jokes.text: It's hard to choose. It all looks so terrible.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at sushi options
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment while still ordering
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer honesty combined with his willingness to proceed anyway

jokes.text: The squid looks like it's been hacked by a blind woodsman. Hang your head in shame.
jokes.setup: Critiquing the sushi presentation
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic condemnation with specific absurd imagery
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific imagery and Homer's tendency toward dramatic overstatement

jokes.text: Oh, boy! This fish is delish!
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying the sushi
jokes.punchline: Simple rhyme showing his enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay but fits Homer's childlike enthusiasm for food

jokes.text: Oh, boy! This fish is delish!
jokes.setup: Homer trying sushi
jokes.punchline: Childish rhyme showing enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple but character-appropriate wordplay

jokes.text: I recommend the raw quail egg. / You're the doctor!
jokes.setup: Waiter recommending exotic food
jokes.punchline: Homer's misplaced trust in restaurant worker as medical authority
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke about misplaced authority and Homer's gullibility

jokes.text: Come on, pal. Fugu me!
jokes.setup: Wanting to try fugu (blowfish)
jokes.punchline: Using 'fugu' as a verb in casual slang way
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that became iconic, perfect Homer linguistic invention

jokes.text: Oh, Miss Krabappel, your hair smells so... clean.
jokes.setup: Chef is supposed to be preparing dangerous fugu
jokes.punchline: He's distracted hitting on a woman with awkward compliment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with the awkward pause and lame pickup line

jokes.text: My skilled hands are busy! You do it!
jokes.setup: Master chef needed for dangerous fugu preparation
jokes.punchline: He refuses because he's busy with his pickup attempt
jokes.explanation: Escalates the danger while showing the chef's misplaced priorities

jokes.text: Poison. Poison. Tasty fish!
jokes.setup: Inexperienced chef trying to cut dangerous fugu
jokes.punchline: His nervous mantra while making potentially fatal mistakes
jokes.explanation: Great tension-building repetition with dark comedy undertones

jokes.text: Fan-fugu-tastic!
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying his potentially deadly meal
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic pun combining 'fantastic' with 'fugu'
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that shows Homer's oblivious enthusiasm in face of danger

jokes.text: 'Try something new. How can it hurt you, Homer?'
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on advice to try new things
jokes.punchline: The ultimate ironic outcome - it literally might kill him
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony callback that perfectly encapsulates the episode's theme

jokes.text: Never heard of a poisoned pork chop!
jokes.setup: Homer regretting trying something new
jokes.punchline: Longing for the safety of his rigid routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier routine obsession, now seen as safety

jokes.text: No need, doc. I can read Marge like a book.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting they tell Marge about the situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident claim about understanding his wife
jokes.explanation: Standard marital humor - Homer's overconfidence in spousal understanding

jokes.text: 24 hours? / Well, 22. I'm sorry I kept you waiting.
jokes.setup: Doctor delivering devastating news about time left to live
jokes.punchline: Casually adjusting downward for appointment delay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation with doctor's matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: when your heart explodes
jokes.setup: Describing how Homer will die
jokes.punchline: Casual medical description of violent death
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy with medical professional's clinical detachment

jokes.text: First, denial. / No way! I'm not dying! / The second is anger. / Why, you little--!
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining stages of death acceptance
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately and perfectly demonstrating each stage
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with Homer's instant demonstration of psychological stages

jokes.text: 'So You're Going to Die.'
jokes.setup: Doctor offering helpful materials
jokes.punchline: Absurdly blunt pamphlet title
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag with bureaucratic approach to death

jokes.text: Hello, Marge. Hello! I'm the one dying, not you.
jokes.setup: Marge is upset about the news
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-centered response to her grief
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness even in tragedy, good character integration

jokes.text: What's that word you use for when you and I...? / When we're intimate? / 'Be intimate with Marge.'
jokes.setup: Homer making his to-do list for last day
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly clinical way of listing marital intimacy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Homer's awkwardness about intimacy

jokes.text: There will be plenty of time for that!
jokes.setup: Marge says he looked peaceful sleeping
jokes.punchline: Homer's response when he literally has no time left
jokes.explanation: Great irony with Homer missing the point about limited time

jokes.text: Number one: Cover for me. Number two: Oh, good idea, boss! Three: It was like that when I got here.
jokes.setup: Homer sharing life wisdom with Bart
jokes.punchline: His 'wisdom' is just workplace survival tactics
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer life philosophy - perfectly captures his work ethic and becomes highly quotable

jokes.text: You son of a--! / And that's how we shave.
jokes.setup: Homer teaching Bart to shave, presumably cuts himself with aftershave
jokes.punchline: Continuing lesson calmly after pain reaction
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer's attempt to maintain dignity during teaching

jokes.text: It was like that when I got here! / That's my boy!
jokes.setup: Bart presumably caused some mishap
jokes.punchline: Uses Homer's life advice immediately, making Homer proud
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with Bart immediately applying Homer's 'wisdom' successfully

jokes.text: Oh, I want to be in that rumba / When the saints go over there / Oh, over there!
jokes.setup: Lisa playing saxophone for her father
jokes.punchline: Mixing up 'When the Saints Go Marching In' with 'Over There'
jokes.explanation: Mild musical joke showing Lisa's musical ability with slight confusion

jokes.text: The joke's on him! I'll be dead by then.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to Flanders' barbecue invitation
jokes.punchline: Homer's grim satisfaction about avoiding social obligation
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy with Homer finding silver lining in his death sentence

jokes.text: Unless you taped over this, you probably wonder about your dad.
jokes.setup: Homer making video message for baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the mundane reality that tapes get reused
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor acknowledging how family videos actually get treated

jokes.text: We never danced the hootchy-koo either.
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting they never did typical father-son activities
jokes.punchline: Bart's absurd addition to the list
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Bart's non-sequitur addition

jokes.text: Gee, Dad, hog my last moments.
jokes.setup: Homer monopolizing their final time together
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic commentary on selfish behavior
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal - child calling out parent's selfishness in crisis

jokes.text: The old guy's a little love-starved.
jokes.setup: Homer being overly affectionate trying to make up for lost time
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual psychological assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Bart's mature observation about his father

jokes.text: When I say give me a ticket, just give me a ticket!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to speed up getting a traffic ticket
jokes.punchline: His impatience making the situation worse
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's impatience backfiring in typical fashion

jokes.text: Look what else your tax dollars pay for.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about taxes paying police salaries
jokes.punchline: Officer showing him jail cell as another 'service'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with officer's sarcastic response to Homer's complaint

jokes.text: Nobody's here! Nobody's here! / Damn those answering machine tapes!
jokes.setup: Homer calling Barney for help
jokes.punchline: Barney's drunk message defeats the purpose of answering machines
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney character moment with his drunk logic about answering machines

jokes.text: Hey, Homer, go to the window. Hey, neighbor! I can see you!
jokes.setup: Barney arriving at jail to help Homer
jokes.punchline: Treating jail visit like casual social call
jokes.explanation: Character-driven misunderstanding - treating serious situation casually

jokes.text: What did you do? Kill a judge?
jokes.setup: Homer needs $50 for bail
jokes.punchline: Barney's assumption about what crime costs that much
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with Barney's extreme assumption

jokes.text: What did you do? Kill a judge?
jokes.setup: Barney learning bail is $50
jokes.punchline: Assuming extreme crime for modest bail amount
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor - assuming worst crime for standard bail

jokes.text: Forty-eight dollars and 70 cents. / We don't usually take rusty money.
jokes.setup: Barney paying Homer's bail
jokes.punchline: Money quality being questionable
jokes.explanation: Character detail humor - Barney's questionable money source

jokes.text: We don't usually take rusty money.
jokes.setup: Barney paying bail with found money
jokes.punchline: The poor condition of Barney's money
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag about Barney's questionable money sources

jokes.text: That's it, baby. Work those ankles!
jokes.setup: Burns at strip club with Smithers
jokes.punchline: His peculiar and innocent fixation on ankles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - his old-fashioned prudishness even in a strip club

jokes.text: Burns! Eat my shorts!
jokes.setup: Homer finally getting to tell his boss off
jokes.punchline: Using Bart's catchphrase for his big moment
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Homer borrowing his son's rebellious phrase

jokes.text: Of all the luck. I almost died without telling the boss to eat my shorts.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on crossing item off his list
jokes.punchline: His priorities about what's important before death
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's skewed priorities

jokes.text: Hey, is there a Butts here? A Seymour Butts? / Hey, everybody. I wanna Seymour Butts!
jokes.setup: Classic prank call setup
jokes.punchline: Moe falling for the name joke
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons running gag - not original but well-executed and quotable

jokes.text: You scum-sucking pus bucket! When I get you, I'm gonna pull out your eyeballs with a corkscrew!
jokes.setup: Moe realizes he's been pranked
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and violent threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moe characterization - creative and disturbing threat

jokes.text: I never told you, but sometimes when I'm at work, I think of you and smile.
jokes.setup: Homer giving sentimental speech to bar patrons
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-sharing with drinking buddies
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Homer's inappropriate emotional display

jokes.text: Not in public! / You'd better be dying!
jokes.setup: Homer hugging Moe and declaring love
jokes.punchline: Moe's masculine discomfort with emotional display
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Moe's typical gruff reaction to sentiment

jokes.text: The clouds are forming... Give me a break.
jokes.setup: Marge reading romantic poem she wrote
jokes.punchline: Homer's impatient interruption of his own eulogy poem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - can't even appreciate his own death poem

jokes.text: I'm Larry King. In the beginning, God created the heaven and the earth.
jokes.setup: Homer listening to Bible on tape for final hours
jokes.punchline: Larry King's distinctive delivery of Genesis
jokes.explanation: Celebrity cameo joke relying on Larry King's recognizable style

jokes.text: That's it. Duke Zeibert's standing by. We'll get some matzo ball soup. I love the Spurs.
jokes.setup: Larry King reading the Bible
jokes.punchline: He seamlessly transitions to his typical random commentary
jokes.explanation: Good Larry King parody showing his stream-of-consciousness style

jokes.text: His drool, it's warm!
jokes.setup: Marge checking if Homer is alive
jokes.punchline: Her relief expressed through gross detail
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that shows Marge's relief in unglamorous way

jokes.text: From now on, I vow to live life to its fullest!
jokes.setup: Homer's profound realization about living fully
jokes.punchline: Sets up for immediate contradiction of his vow
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for Homer's inevitable return to laziness

jokes.text: [Homer immediately watching bowling on TV]
jokes.setup: Homer just vowed to live life to the fullest
jokes.punchline: Immediately returns to passive TV watching
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual punchline showing Homer's instant return to form - excellent structure and character integration
